name: Vértice Geodésico de La Lastra – circular desde Cabrejas del Pinar
description: This moderate 7.5-mile (12.0 km) circular hike from Cabrejas del Pinar leads to the Geodetic Vertex of La Lastra with panoramic views.
region_intro: Hiking around Cabrejas Del Pinar offers routes through diverse landscapes, characterized by extensive pine forests, natural springs, and tranquil reservoir areas. The region, located in Soria, Spain, features gentle hills and varied terrain suitable for exploration. Trails often follow river courses or lead to scenic viewpoints, providing a mix of shaded paths and open vistas. Best hiking trails around Cabrejas Del Pinar The most popular hiking route is Route of La Fuentona de Muriel – round trip, a 1.9 miles (3.0 km) trail that takes 48 minutes to complete, leading to a significant natural spring. Another top favourite among local hikers is Playa Pita Loop Trail, an easy 2.6 miles (4.2 km) path. This route offers views of the reservoir and surrounding forest. Local hikers also love the Abejar Springs and Viewpoints Loop, a 6.5 miles (10.5 km) trail leading through pine forests and offering elevated perspectives, often completed in about 2 hours 58 minutes. Hiking around Cabrejas Del Pinar is defined by pine forests, natural springs, and reservoir paths. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and more challenging ability levels. The routes in Cabrejas Del Pinar are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 70 reviews. More than 600 hikers have used komoot to explore Cabrejas Del Pinar's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Cabrejas Del Pinar?
A: There are over 35 hiking trails around Cabrejas Del Pinar, offering a variety of options for different skill levels. This includes 13 easy routes, 20 moderate trails, and 3 more challenging hikes.

Q: Are there any easy, family-friendly hikes in Cabrejas Del Pinar?
A: Yes, Cabrejas Del Pinar offers several easy, family-friendly options. A great choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1379179877/playa-pita-loop-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Playa Pita Loop Trail&lt;/a&gt;, an easy 4.2 km path with views of the reservoir. Another accessible option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1056692533/route-of-la-fuentona-de-muriel-round-trip" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Route of La Fuentona de Muriel – round trip&lt;/a&gt;, which is 3.0 km long and leads to a significant natural spring.

Q: What kind of natural attractions can I see along the hiking trails?
A: The region is rich in natural beauty. You can explore the unique karst spring of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4709888" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Fuentona de Muriel&lt;/a&gt;, or enjoy the scenic views around &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5315870" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Playa Pita&lt;/a&gt; on the Cuerda del Pozo Reservoir. Many trails also lead through extensive pine forests and past natural springs.

Q: Are there any circular walks in Cabrejas Del Pinar?
A: Yes, many trails in Cabrejas Del Pinar are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1063217868/abejar-springs-and-viewpoints-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Abejar Springs and Viewpoints Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 10.5 km route that takes you through pine forests and offers elevated perspectives. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1062288644/vertice-geodesico-de-la-lastra-circular-desde-cabrejas-del-pinar" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vértice Geodésico de La Lastra – circular desde Cabrejas del Pinar&lt;/a&gt;, a 12.0 km moderate hike.

Q: What are the typical trail conditions and terrain like?
A: Hiking around Cabrejas Del Pinar features diverse landscapes, primarily characterized by extensive pine forests, natural springs, and tranquil reservoir areas. The terrain often includes gentle hills and varied paths, with trails following river courses or leading to scenic viewpoints, providing a mix of shaded sections and open vistas.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Cabrejas Del Pinar?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 70 reviews. Hikers often praise the tranquil pine forests, the beauty of the natural springs, and the well-maintained paths that offer a peaceful outdoor experience.

Q: Are there any historical landmarks or points of interest near the hiking routes?
A: Beyond the natural beauty, you can find historical points of interest. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2721569" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Submerged Roman Bridge of Vinuesa&lt;/a&gt; is a notable landmark, and the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/294710" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ucero Castle&lt;/a&gt; is also within reach, offering a glimpse into the region's past.

Q: Are there any longer, more challenging hikes for experienced hikers?
A: While many routes are easy to moderate, there are options for longer hikes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1063869136/senda-de-las-cabras-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Senda de las Cabras Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 10.6 km route that offers a good distance for those looking for a longer outing. There are also 3 routes classified as difficult for those seeking a greater challenge.

Q: Is it possible to combine hiking with water activities?
A: Yes, the proximity to the Cuerda del Pozo Reservoir, particularly around &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5315870" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Playa Pita&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2721568" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vinuesa&lt;/a&gt;, makes it possible to combine hiking with water-based activities like swimming or simply relaxing by the water after a walk.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Cabrejas Del Pinar?
A: The region is enjoyable for hiking across multiple seasons. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant natural colors, while summer provides warm, sunny days, often with shaded paths through the pine forests. Winter can also be beautiful, though some trails might be affected by snow or colder conditions.

Q: Are there any trails that lead to viewpoints?
A: Yes, several trails are designed to offer elevated perspectives of the surrounding landscape. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1063217868/abejar-springs-and-viewpoints-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Abejar Springs and Viewpoints Loop&lt;/a&gt;, for instance, is specifically known for providing excellent views over the pine forests and the wider region.
tips: In La Corona, we find two traditional sheepfolds. Near these, there is a small cave, can you find it?
At this point we find the Geodesic Vertex of La Lastra. Located at an altitude of 1,342 meters, we have magnificent views of the landscape that surrounds us.

faqs.question: Where does the Vértice Geodésico de La Lastra circular trail start?
faqs.answer: This circular route starts and ends in Cabrejas del Pinar. While specific parking details aren't provided, it's generally advisable to look for designated parking areas within the village or near the main access points for hiking trails.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this hike?
faqs.answer: The trail traverses varied landscapes typical of the Soria region, including extensive pine and juniper groves. While specific details aren't exhaustive, expect paths that can range from dirt tracks through forests to more open, elevated areas as you approach the geodetic vertex. Be prepared for potentially uneven paths, as is common on moderate hikes.

faqs.question: What kind of views can I expect from the Vértice Geodésico de La Lastra?
faqs.answer: The primary reward of this hike is the magnificent panoramic views from the Geodetic Vertex of La Lastra. Located at an altitude of 1342 meters, this strategic point offers expansive vistas of the surrounding Soria landscape, including its characteristic pine forests and rolling terrain.

faqs.question: Is the Vértice Geodésico de La Lastra trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While there are no explicit restrictions mentioned, most trails in natural areas like this are generally dog-friendly. However, it's always best to keep your dog on a leash, especially in areas where wildlife might be present or near livestock, and to carry waste bags to clean up after your pet.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this route?
faqs.answer: The Soria region offers beautiful hiking conditions in spring and autumn, with pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery. Summer can also be enjoyable, though it's wise to start early to avoid the midday heat. Winters can bring colder temperatures and potentially snow, which would add to the difficulty but offer a different kind of beauty.

faqs.question: Are there any specific landmarks or points of interest along the way?
faqs.answer: Yes, besides the main destination, the Geodetic Vertex of La Lastra, you'll also pass by the Traditional Majadas of La Corona, offering a glimpse into local heritage and architecture.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay an entrance fee for this hike?
faqs.answer: No, there is no mention of any permits or entrance fees required to hike the Vértice Geodésico de La Lastra circular trail. Access to natural areas in this region is generally free and open to the public.

faqs.question: What should I bring for this moderate hike?
faqs.answer: Given its moderate difficulty and potential for varied weather, it's recommended to bring sturdy hiking boots, sufficient water, snacks, and layers of clothing. A waterproof jacket is advisable, as the weather can change, and you should be prepared for potential rain.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: This trail is classified as moderate. While it's not overly strenuous with an elevation gain of around 250 meters over 12 kilometers, it does require a reasonable level of fitness. Beginners who are accustomed to some physical activity and are prepared for a few hours of walking should find it manageable, but it's not a flat, easy stroll.

faqs.question: Are there any accommodation or dining options near Cabrejas del Pinar?
faqs.answer: Cabrejas del Pinar is a small village, and while specific establishments aren't detailed, it's reasonable to expect local cafes, restaurants, or rural accommodation options. It's always a good idea to check in advance, especially during off-peak seasons, for availability and opening hours.

faqs.question: How can I get to Cabrejas del Pinar by public transport?
faqs.answer: Information on direct public transport to Cabrejas del Pinar for hikers is not readily available. It's likely that bus services connect larger towns in Soria province, and you might need to arrange local transport from there. Driving is often the most convenient way to reach the trailhead.
